title: Friends - 1003 - The One With Ross' Tan

lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock and Wendy Knoller

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, you and Rachel tonight, huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. It's actually our first official date

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! So tonight may be the night! You're nervous?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Naa, no. This is the part I'm actually good at.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What must it be like not to be crippled by fear and self-loathing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's OK!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How can you be so confident?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I... I know exactly what I'm gonna do!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really? Like you have a routine?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no no no no. See. Each woman is different.You have to appreciate their uniqueness.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I do six things! First, I look deep in her eyes. Then, I kiss her. Next I take my hand and I softly graze her thigh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean like this?
lines.stage_direction: he starts touching his thigh in a funny and awkard way

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: NO! Not like that, no no. No, like this.
lines.stage_direction: He starts lightly grazing Chandler's thigh

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I see what you mean, that's quite nice.
lines.stage_direction: They look at each other, both embarassed

lines.text: More foosball?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...and beer!!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Hey Pheebs!

lines.text: Hey Phoebe!

lines.text: Oh, you won't believe who moved back to town.

lines.text: I know, Amanda! Ah! She called me too! She's the worst!

lines.text: Who's Amanda?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's this girl who used to live in the building before you did. Then she moved to England and she picked up this fake British accent. On the machine this is her message. "Monica, darling! It's Amanda calling!"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you trying to do a British accent?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler gets pedicures!

lines.text: Just so I know, how many more of those can I expect?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know what Amanda said to me when she got me on the phone? "Oh, so sorry to catch you on your Mo-Bile!" If-if you don't wanna get me on my mo-Bile, don't call me on my mo-Bile!"

lines.text: Ugh, Let's just cut her out!

scenes.location: At the tanning salon. Ross and a male assistant are walking through a hall

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Alright Mr. Geller! Right this way! So, how dark do you wanna be? We have one, two or three.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well... I like how you look, what are you?

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Puerto Rican.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Two, I think a two.

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: You've got to face the red light. When the red light goes on the spraying is about to start so close your eyes. When the spraying stops, count to five. Pat yourself down to avoid drip marks then turn around so we can get your back. Got it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Spray, count, pat, then turn, spray, count and pat.

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Wow, you catch on quick.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I have a PhD, so...
lines.stage_direction: assistant walk out, not impressed by this statement Ross takes his bathrobe off and he enters the tanning booth. He stands up in front of the red light and the sprayer starts and sprays his face and torso

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One Mississipi, two Mississipi, Three Mis... WAIT! WAIT! I'm not-I've not finished counting!!
lines.stage_direction: the sprayer starts again, spraying him in the face and torso again he leaves the booth the assistant enters the room

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You sprayed my front twice!

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: You've never turned?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I barely even got to three Mississippi.

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Mississippi? I said count to five'!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mississippilesly? Well, how bad is it?

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Ain't that bad yet, but it keeps getting darker for the next four hours.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, how dark is it gonna get?

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: You got sprayed with two two' s and...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm a four?

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Yeah, but you're back's a zero. You're gonna wanna even that out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really!

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: You might wanna get back in there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok!
lines.stage_direction: annoyed The assistant leaves and Ross goes back in the spray-on tan booth and turns his back to the spray nozzles, facing the back wall

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait, wait a minute, there's no light on the back wall! How do I know when it's gonna start? Hello? Ah, oh, ah! Ah! The same thing happened again!
lines.stage_direction: he slowly turns and the spraying begins, on his face he turns, but then he turns again and is sprayed in the front again he spits and angrily goes out of the spray-on tan booth and the assistant enters the room

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: You got two more twos?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm an eight!

lines.text: Oh my God, that place has the creamiest frosting! I use to hitchhike there when I was a kid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, anyway, they make these great novelty cakes, in all different shapes, and if you give them a photo, they’ll copy it in icing!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, did you do a picture of Emma?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! On a cake shaped like a bunny.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Rach? Does this bakery by any chance also bake erotic cakes? Say for bachelorette parties?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, what are you talking about? oh! Oh my God! They put my baby’s face on a penis!

lines.speaker: Phoebe (sees the cake)
lines.text: oh! Now it’s a party!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why you guys this isn't funny, all right? If I wanted this cake to be a disaster I would have baked it myself!

lines.speaker: Joey (staring at the cake)
lines.text: Uh.. is it ok that I still think it looks delicious?

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Jack, look at this.

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: I know what you're thinking Judy, the resemblance is uncanny!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am this close to tugging on my testicles again.

lines.speaker: Rachel (on the phone)
lines.text: No, no, this is not what I ordered. Ok? I went all the way to New Jersey so that I could have the perfect cake for my daughter’s birthday and I need a bunny cake, right now!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ask them if it would be faster if we cut the baby’s face off the penis, so we can put it on the bunny. . That is a weird sentence!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Believe you me! I am going to bring this cake back, I don't even want it in my home... Joey, don't touch it!!
lines.stage_direction: Turns towards the cake and sees Joey trying to take a piece and yells at him

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm so confused!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, yes. I still want my daughters picture, but on a bunny cake. Yellow cake, chocolate frosting with nuts!
lines.stage_direction: speaking to the person on the phone again

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To be fair this one does have nuts.

scenes.scene_description: Joey’s Apartment. Ross is filming his parents
Time lapse

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Mon, that was really nice of you to loan Rachel your car so she could go and get the cake.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. So nice of her to pull my hair, 'till I dropped the key!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you know what? While we're waiting, you guys could tape your message to Emma for her 18th birthday, huh? Ok!
lines.stage_direction: takes the camcorder and points it at Monica and Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi Emma! It's the year 2020. Are you still enjoying your nap?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're Aunt Monica and uncle Chandler, by the way. You may not recognize us, because we haven't spoken to your parents in seventeen years!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We used to be married, but then we missed a weekend away together and things kind of unraveled. Because of you! Happy Birthday.
lines.stage_direction: Ross looks disappointed and switches the camcorder off

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, Rachel promised it would be over by now. We seriously have to go, if we want to get to Vermont. I called them and the last train leaves in a half hour.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And you know, I have a massage client soon.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You guys, just please.. a little bit longer. I promise, Rachel will be back with the cake any minute. Monica, remember.. the frosting? huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, 5 more minutes.

scenes.scene_description: Ross, Chandler and Monica are in the kitchen area

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello? Oh no! What happened? Ok ok, where are you? . Ok, I'll be right there.
lines.stage_direction: Into receiver listens listens some more Grabs a pen and starts writing Puts the phone down

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Was that Emma? Is she up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, Rachel got pulled over for speeding. She forgot her licence so now I have to bring it to her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if you're leaving, I'm definitely gonna go.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Wait! If anybody gets to go.. it's us We've been complaining the longest!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, wait ! You guys, no, no, you can't leave! Rachel already feels bad that the cake's messed up. How do you think she's gonna feel when she comes back here and all you guys are gone?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know! You'll tell us on Monday!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey! You're in charge, ok? You make sure nobody leaves!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Got it! Hey hey hey! Where do you think you're going?
lines.stage_direction: Goes towards the front door and stands with his back to it to Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To the bathroom!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, well the rest of you get comfortable, ok, because we're gonna be here for a li... Wait a minute, there is a window in there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! He's not getting away that easy!
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe and Joey run towards the bathroom and enter

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing? Get the hell out of here!
lines.stage_direction: Yelling Phoebe and Joey come out looking shocked

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, that one did not have Emma's face on it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it did not.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Well, we better get going, it's late. Jack's not allowed to drive at night anymore. He has trouble staying in his lane.

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: Last winter I went up on a church lawn and drove right through a manger scene. The papers thought it was a hate crime.

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Anyway, it was lovely seeing you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok!

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Bye... Bye dear.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nighty-night!

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Nighty-night!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bye, bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey? How could you just let them leave?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey, hey! I'm not gonna mess with Jack, he's a great man, he fought for our country.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, he didn't! He pretended to be a Quaker to get out of Korea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Well that's it! He's the last one to go. I'm locking you guys in.
lines.stage_direction: Shocked turns the bolts of the door, thereby locking it

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do know, I can just turn them the other way around, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! I forgot you used to live here!

scenes.scene_description: The phone rings, Ross picks up

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: Joey! It's Estelle!
lines.stage_direction: on the other side of the line

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: So, how did your audition go today?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What audition?

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: The one I told you about last week?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? You never said anything about an audition!

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: Let me start over. I just got a call about an audition. I think you can still make it. It's down at the Astor Theatre and you need to have a monologue prepared.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A monologue? I don't have.. I got it. Aah! so... I'm gonna take off!
lines.stage_direction: sees the book he was reading before for his "dramatic reading" hangs up announces to the room

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wh.. what? No wait, you don't get to leave! I've got a massage client waiting outside my door any minute!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, and we've paid for a room, that we're supposed to be in right now!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, oh, ok now... Only one of us has to stay with Emma. Ok, and as the person who realized that, I get to go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! no! Let's figure out a fair way to decide who's staying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! I got it! Ok, everyone pick a number from one to ten. Alright? Whoever gets the highest number gets to go first.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, ten.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, Monica picks ten, I call nine! Anyone else?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, lets just draw straws.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or.. we could flip a coin, and then multiply the..

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm begging you stop.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Ok, how about this
lines.text: We got wind up toys for Emma for her birthday. We can make them race, and whoever comes in last, stays!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!! Let's do that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That sounds more fun than the thing we were going to do in Vermont!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Everybody get your toys!
lines.stage_direction: They all run toward the table with toys

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok! I want the dolphin!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thats a bear.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm too excited!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, you get the bear, uhm, Joey, you get the robot, and Chandler and I get the dog. Ok, and the race is going to go from here to here. Now the one who comes in last, stays!
lines.stage_direction: Takes two cups and marks the start and finish lines with them

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, ladies and gentlemen, wind your toys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, on your mark... Get set... GO!!!

scenes.scene_description: The phone rings, Joey picks up

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Go! Go!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on robot!

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Phoebe and Joey release their wind-up toys.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you barking at?

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's bear takes the lead, followed by Joey's robot, and far behind is Chandler and Monica's dog, which walks a few paces, stops and starts barking, sits, walks again, and so on...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wha... How the hell is that gonna help?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I bought you. How did I forget that that's all you do?

scenes.scene_description: While Phoebe's bear is still in the lead, it is now closely followed by Joey's robot. Chandler and Monica's dog however, sits down, barking... and does a backflip.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Way to go robot!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good job Alan!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, good race you guys.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, see you later!

scenes.scene_description: Meanwhile the race has been won by Phoebe's bear, followed by Joey's robot

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No, no... wait! We didn't lose. The rules clearly stated that the last one to cross the finish line was the loser. Well, our dog never crossed the finish line, so technically...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They left.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Phoebe leave for the door
Monica turns to see the closed door.

scenes.location: The freeway. Rachel is driving Monica's Porsche

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, there isn't time to go to the bakery. We're just gonna come home... Everybody left? Alright, well just tell Emma we're gonna be there as soon as we can. Bye...
lines.stage_direction: into the phone emotionally She hangs up, closes her phone, turns around and puts it in her bag which is in the back of the car. While doing this and not looking at the road, she turns the steering wheel by accident, which makes the car swerve.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: RACHEL!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OOH! God! Sorry!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe they gave you a ticket. You're such a good driver.

scenes.scene_description: The freeway. Rachel is driving Monica's Porsche, while using her mobile phone. Ross is sitting next to her.

scenes.location: The freeway. Rachel is driving Monica's Porsche

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Emma's awake.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't believe this. This is her first birthday. She's awake. We're not even there. Everybody left. We still have this stupid obscene cake.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, maybe I can fix that, you know. Try to turn it into something else.

scenes.scene_description: Ross fastens his seat belt.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, why do you even bother? I already ruined her first birthday... And do you know how important these early experiences are Ross? Very! According to the back cover of that book that you gave me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, she's not going to remember this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I guess... Oh, I just had such an idea of what this day would be like, you know? Emma laughing and everybody gathered around her cake singing "Happy Birthday". Then we would all go into... HEY GET OUT OF THE ROAD YOU STUPID STUDENT DRIVER!!! They have to learn!
lines.stage_direction: shakes her head honks furiously, and Ross looks at her in disbelief and Rachel looks at him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's not bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Look what... you made it into a bunny. How did you do that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I just made these two things uhm... cheeks. And then I split this to make ears.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I'm very impressed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Some can sing, some can dance. I apparently can turn phallic cakes into woodland creatures.

scenes.scene_description: he opens the box

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now another way to organise your stuffed animals, is by size.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry, is this a game for Emma or for Monica?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Game?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Emma, you even know it's your birthday today? You're one! One-year-old, that's little.
lines.stage_direction: With his index finger he shows her how old she is. Emma also points her finger and babbles...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh! That's my girl!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's how old you are.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did I teach her that? Did I just... impart wisdom?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ooh, I want one...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me too...

scenes.scene_description: Rachel and Joey's apartment. Chandler, Monica and Emma are sitting on the floor, and Emma's stuffed animals are lined up in front of them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's no-one around. Why don't we just take this one?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...and head to Canada!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was kidding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wasn't. Let's get going!

scenes.scene_description: There's a couple of seconds of silence. Then Chandler looks around...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, are Ross and Rachel back?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh good, I didn't miss the party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about your massage client?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just felt so bad, missing this. So I just slipped him a little something, you know. As long as I'm back in five or six hours, it will be alright.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe enters the apartment again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, if Ross and Rachel ask, I've been here the whole time. THE WHOLE TIME!

scenes.scene_description: Joey comes running into the apartment, out of breath.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I've been here the whole time!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, we just saw you come in. You ran past us on the stairs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't care that you left. I'm just glad that you're here. Thanks you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, guys! Come on! You gotta see what Emma just did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Emma, how old are you? How old are you today?
lines.stage_direction: holds up his index finger again

lines.speaker: Emma
lines.text: Wo-ish.

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Rachel enter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Emma, that's right! You're that many!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! Our daughter's a genius! Rach, this means...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: NO, no science camp!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damnit! I'll put a candle on the cake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!... Oh and Emma, look at your stuffed animals lined up so neatly!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, here we go! Emma's first birthday cake... Well hey... well, blow out the candle. Come on Emma.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let's do it, come on!

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Rachel gasp

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's wrong? Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, nothing! These are happy tears! This is just what I wanted.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, you made it into a bunny.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is wrong with me. It looked more delicious when it was a penis. ENDING CREDITS

scenes.scene_description: Rachel wipes away some tears.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, you ready?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A-a-and... record.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Hi Emma. Well, your first birthday is over, and it was really...
lines.stage_direction: they both look into the camera, and Ross waves. There's a lot of yelling and screaming coming from the hallway, and they get up to look at what's the noise all about. lines.text: Go, go, go Alan! Run you, hairy bastard! end

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it’s the worst way to propose!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Excuse me... .
lines.stage_direction: looks strangely shocked he leaves, then Phoebe realizes what she did

scenes.scene_description: Madison Square Garden. Mike and Phoebe are walking to their seats.

scenes.location: Monica’s apartment. Joey enters the room

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, finished my recommendation. Here. And I think you’ll be very, very happy. It’s the longest I ever spent on a computer without looking at porn.
lines.stage_direction: he hands it over to Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t... uh... understand.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Some of the words are a little too sophisticated for ya?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It doesn’t make any sense.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course it does! It’s smart! I used the the-saurus!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: On every word?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yep!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, what was this sentence originally?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, ‘They are warm, nice, people with big hearts’.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And that became ‘they are humid prepossessing Homo Sapiens with full sized aortic pumps...?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah and hey, I really mean it, dude.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Joey, I don’t think we can use this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, because you signed it baby kangaroo Tribbiani . Hey, why don’t you stop worrying about sounding smart and just be yourself!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know what? You don’t need a thesaurus, just write from here, your full sized aortic pump.

scenes.scene_description: Monica’s apartment. Joey enters the room

scenes.location: The corridor. Amy knocks Joey’s door and Rachel opens the door

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Amy, hi!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I took your advice, I left Myron.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, good for you!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I know! I'm Erin Brockovich!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes you are! Oh, I am so proud of you!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Thank you! So, can I stay with you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But Erin Brockovich had her own house.
lines.stage_direction: Joey comes out of Monica’s apartment and sees Rachel and Amy but does not notice the huge amount of bags

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, look who’s back! Why do you have bags? RACH, WHY DOES SHE HAVE BAGS?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, I’m staying with you guys!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: We’re gonna be roomies! Come on!
lines.stage_direction: she snaps her finger and points at Joey, snaps her fingers again and points to the bags

scenes.scene_description: The corridor. Amy knocks Joey’s door and Rachel opens the door.

scenes.location: Joey’s apartment. Rachel is in the living room and Joey comes out of his room

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You slept out here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah... Amy kept kicking me in her sleep yelling ‘Myron, get off!’

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But uhm, we're getting rid of her, right? Rach, please tell me we’re getting rid of her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, I can’t do that!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, come on! Last night I was finishing off a pizza and she said "Uoh oh oh, a moment on the lips, forever on the hips!" I don’t need that kind of talk in my house!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well Joey, uhm look, I know that she’s difficult, but I think it’s really good that she’s here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 'Cause we will appreciate it more when she’s gone?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's just... look, you know, when I first moved to the city I was a lot like her! I was spoiled, self-centered and you guys really took care of me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, Monica made us!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, uhm... whatever, I have really appreciated it, 'cause I don’t think I would be the person that I am today if it wasn’t for you guys. See, I wanna help Amy the way you guys helped me. And I know it’s gonna take patience, but that’s ok.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeaah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Amy, that’s what I was supposed to wear today, that’s why I hung it on the door.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh, sweety, you can’t pull this off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Amy, you know what? I was thinking that maybe now it'd be a good time for us to sit down and, you know, talk about your future.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh, I can’t, honey. I’m gonna go get my eyebrows shaped. I am not happy. Oh... sure you wanna eat that?
lines.stage_direction: points at her eyebrows to Joey who has a pizza box in his hands

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’M CURVY, AND I LIKE IT!

scenes.scene_description: Joey’s apartment. Rachel is in the living room and Joey comes out of his room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi. I just had the worst anniversary ever.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I doubt that! Tell her about us last year.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, well, I bought Chandler a five hundred dollar watch and he wrote me a rap song.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Seriously?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Word!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, mine was worse than that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what happened?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We were at the game, and this guy proposed to his girlfriend on the big screen thing...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that is so tacky.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, that’s what I said, but it turns out, Mike was planning on proposing to me that same way last night!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, Mike was gonna propose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, that’s huge!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, do you wanna marry him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I really do! Yes, but, after I dumped on the way he was gonna propose to me, I don’t think he’s ever gonna ask again! I mean, I said no in Barbados and now this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She's right! If I were a guy and... Did I just say if I were a guy..?
lines.stage_direction: stops himself mid-sentence...everyone stares at him

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe you don't need him to propose to you, maybe you can propose to him!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I don't know, I don't know, isn't that a little desperate?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I proposed to Chandler! Alright, moving on...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I don't think it was desperate, I think it was amazing!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, do you think I should propose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think it could be kind of great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Absolutely! You'll love the feeling! There's nothing like it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, ok, so how should I do it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How about at a game, on the big screen?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uuuh!! How about at a Footlocker?
lines.stage_direction: Sarcastically claps her hands together, faking excitement

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? what? He obviously thinks that's a nice way to be proposed to, plus he'd never suspect it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, that does make sense. Ok, now, would... would you two like that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sounds good to me... but what would a guy think?

scenes.scene_description: Monica’s Apartment. Rachel, Ross, Monica and Chandler are there, Phoebe enters the room

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Nana is on the phone
lines.stage_direction: To Rachel Hands the phone over to Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! That's interesting, since she died seven years ago!!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: She did? Who got her condo in Boca?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello? Oh, it's our nanny! Hi! Oh... God! I hope you feel better! Ok, bye! That's Molly, she's sick. Can you watch Emma today?
lines.stage_direction: Into the phone announces to Amy goes back to the phone conversation Hangs up To Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I can't. I have back-to-back classes. Did Molly say what she had? Because my throat's been hurting?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Menstrual cramps.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't think that's what this is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can any of you watch Emma?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, sorry sweety..

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I've got work and then I'm proposing..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great, shoot, what are we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, I can do it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, actually...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, can I talk to you for a sec.?
lines.stage_direction: He interrupts her immediately, and drags her by her arm to the other side of the room

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um, I do not want her baby-sitting our child.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, for one thing, she keeps calling her Ella!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wha.. well, Ella's a nice name!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, we'll call the next one Ella.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wha... the next one?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, um... I don't want her watching our baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I am trying to help her become a better person. This is a huge breakthrough for her! She just offered to do something for another human being!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I... I don't know..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I'm telling you, she's giving up getting her eyebrows shaped to do this alright? Do you understand how important that is in our world?
lines.stage_direction: points at her own to emphasize the word

scenes.scene_description: Amy walks in carrying a phone handset

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: um... listen, I couldn't help but overhear... 'cause I was trying to... Listen, let me do this alright? I really wanna help you guys out, and plus Rachel's been so wonderful to me...

scenes.scene_description: Amy approaches from behind

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh! Great! So how much does it pay?
lines.stage_direction: very excited Ross just gives up and leaves

scenes.scene_description: Rachel looks at Ross and her agrees silently

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe! How's the second draft of the letter coming?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great, I'm finished! In fact, I just dropped it off at the agency.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Joey is inside, Chandler and Monica walk in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You dropped it off?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can we read it? Can you print out another copy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No can do amigo. No, I didn't use the computer. Felt more personal to hand-write it.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler and Monica look even more shocked

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You hand-wrote it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and don't worry. I didn't try to sound smart at all ! See ya later!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, oh my God, that letter is gonna go in our file! We're never gonna get a kid. No, we're gonna be one of those old couples that collects orchids or has a lot of birds!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's ok, it's ok. You know what? I'll just call the agency and tell them to throw out the letter.
lines.stage_direction: Takes out his mobile starts dialing

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello, this is Chandler Bing. Somebody just dropped off a handwritten recommendation letter, and.. Uh-huh... Uh-huh... okay... thank you. Good-bye. .
lines.stage_direction: on the phone listens hangs up looking very confused

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh, we're screwed, aren't we? You know what? Just tell me on the way to the bird store.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They loved it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They thought it was very smart of us to have a child write the recommendation letter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They thought Joey was a child?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She guessed 8, 9, based on his drawings .

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica look shocked

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, what did you guys do today?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ella wanted to go out, so we went shopping and got some sushi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That sounds like fun.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Yeah, not really. Babies are dull.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Hi, how's my girl?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I'm fine! And, I got you a present for letting me stay with you. Ready?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ta-daaa!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You pierced her ears!?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Doesn't it make her nose look smaller?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel looks at Emma

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You pierced her ears? How could you do this without telling me?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, if I had told you, then it wouldn't have been surprise, now would it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think she looks cute. ... but I am wrong!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, Oh my God, here comes Ross. He's gonna flip out.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Why, did something happen to his falafel cart?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh.
lines.stage_direction: takes the hat and covers Emma's head and half her face with it

scenes.scene_description: Rachel looks very shocked

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: Monica and Chandler
lines.text: Hi Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Emma. Oh, why is she wearing her hat so low? She can barely see. .
lines.stage_direction: Wants to take the hat off, but Rachel tries to stop him

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nah, I don't really want her to see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because there are so many terrible sights in this world.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Like war. Or that thing in Joey's refrigerator. Remember? It was in a milk carton but it looked like meat?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come here Oh! There she is! Hi!

scenes.scene_description: Ross enters

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?
lines.stage_direction: sees how strangely Monica, Chandler and Rachel are looking at him

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! What... . Please tell me those are clip-ons.
lines.stage_direction: Back to Emma Looks at her Moves the stroller away from him so he can get a better look at her. He looks at her confused. Finally he realizes the difference and gasps

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, they're real!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did she do this to her? I told you we shouldn't have left Emma with her!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I know, and you were right Ross. You are soo irresponsible I am never letting you baby-sit ever again!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Hey you know what, this kid needs me, okay? She needs to have a cool fun aunt!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm a cool, fun aunt!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: O-Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Monica can be cool and fun at organized indoor projects!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't believe this. All I wanted to do was help you try to figure out what to do with your life and this is how you repay me?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, I don't need you to help me, because I already know what I'm going to do with my life.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah? Since when?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Since today... I am going to be a baby stylist.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?
lines.stage_direction: looks at Ross and then at Amy again in disbelief

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's not a thing !

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, it should be. I'm gonna help babies learn how to accessorize, what colors to wear, what clothes are slimming...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Babies don't care if they're slim.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Enter Amy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Amy, I ju... I just... I just wanna...

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: What? What are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No more falafel for you!
lines.stage_direction: pointing at Amy, shouting Amy looks at Ross, angrily. Rachel clearly doesn't understand what he meant and looks at Ross who gestures "later".

lines.speaker: Mike

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I think you should wait.

lines.text: Why?

lines.text: Well, if you don't... if you don't hold it in, you don't get all the nutrients.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Knicks fans, please turn your attention to the big screen on the scoreboard. Someone has a special question to ask.
lines.stage_direction: We see the screen where it says: "Mike will you marry me?" and then we see Phoebe and Mike on the screen. Phoebe stands up and kneels in front of Mike.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike Hannigan... will you marry me?

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Get a load of this... She's proposing to him . Guess we know who wears the pants in that family.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's not very enlightened!
lines.stage_direction: There's booing around them, and Mike sinks in his chair, holding his hand in above his eyes, hoping no-one would recognize him

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, hey! Boo us? Boo you!

scenes.scene_description: Madison Square Garden. Phoebe and Mike are watching the game. There's cheering, but Phoebe seems distracted.

scenes.location: Rachel and Joey's. Joey is in the kitchen and the telephone rings

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello? Yeah, this is Joey Tribbiani... Oh, hi! Well, I'm glad you liked my letter... No my mommy and daddy aren't home right now... Okay, bye bye. She was nice!
lines.stage_direction: picks up the phone looks puzzled hangs up to himself

scenes.scene_description: Rachel and Joey's. Joey is in the kitchen and the telephone rings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, get Amy's bags, she is moving out!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoo-hoo!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You're kicking me out?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You put holes in my baby's ears!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Yeah well, at least now people will know she is a girl !

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't believe I ever even tried to help you. You are so beyond help.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You know what? Ever since I got here, you have been nothing but negative.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You didn't want me to marry the old guy with the great apartment. Then, I tried to help your daughter to de-emphasize her flaws And suddenly I am the bad guy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, where are those bags?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She has a lot of crap!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You know what? When I moved in here I thought: This is gonna be so great. Just us sisters, back together again like when we were kids, except without that stupid Jill... Oh! Who has gotten fat by the way...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Seriously?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: hm-mmm... Mom said she gained like fifteen pounds.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hips or thighs?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ass and face.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh my God! I thought she was on Atkins.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: She was. Carbs found her... See, this is what I wanted. Two sisters, talking about real stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I can give you that.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You can?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. I just, I kept trying to make you a better person, but you're... you're already a pretty perfect version of what you are.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Thank you. I've got to admit, Emma does look cute.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did you just say Emma?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ugh, I'm sorry... Ella.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel and Amy enter

scenes.location: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That woman at the game didn't know what she was talking about. Mike, obviously you have balls.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: But please, let's just forget the whole thing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I would love it. Consider it forgotten... But just so you know... however and whenever you decide to propose, I promise I'll say yes. Whether... whether, you know, it is in a basketball game, or in sky writing, or you know, like some lame guy in a cheesy movie who hides it in the cake.
lines.stage_direction: the waiter puts a piece of cake on the table

scenes.scene_description: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant.

scenes.location: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's in the cake, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Where else would lame Mr. No Balls hide it?
lines.stage_direction: puts on a fake smile he takes the ring from the cake, and cleans it with a napkin

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's the matter with me? How do I keep ruining this? I'm sorry, I'm sorry!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No! It's my fault. I keep trying to propose in these stupid ways and all I wanna do is tell you that I love you and I want to spend the rest of my life with you.

scenes.scene_description: Mike's face changes from happy to sad, and he looks at the cake, disappointed.

scenes.location: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I'm gonna do this now .

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe has this weird, anxious, nervous look on her face

scenes.location: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Phoebe, I...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait! Oh wait! Oh no!
lines.stage_direction: she takes off a ring that was already on her left ring finger. After that Mike starts to kneel again, but then... She was wearing rings on all her fingers and her thumb, and takes all of these off.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ready?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Phoebe, I love you. There's no-one else in the world I would ask to marry me... three times. But I wanna take care of you, have babies with you, and grow old with you... Phoebe Buffay, will you marry me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes!

scenes.scene_description: Mike starts to kneel in front of Phoebe.

scenes.location: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I love you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love you more!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Not possible! She's gonna be Mrs. No Balls.

scenes.location: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant
scenes.scene_description: They kiss again, and Phoebe looks at the ring.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So how is the uhm... baby styling business going?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Not that great. It's almost if people don't want to hear that their babies are ugly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's shocking!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh! It's Ross... Hey Ross! Hello-oo Ross! He's rude! THE END

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: God!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: So beautiful.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know, isn't she?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: No, I was talking about your bedding.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. What's your news, Amy?

lines.text: Oh! Um... Well... I'm getting married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Oh my God! To who?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: This guy! He has a killer apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A-And??

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: A-And it's on Fifth. And the elevator opens up right into the living room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, what's he like?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh! He's ok. Do you remember my old boyfriend Mark?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: It's his dad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh... wow, so he's gotta be...

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Old? Yeah! But he travels a lot, so he's hardly ever there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sweety, I gotta tell ya... it sounds a little bit like you like the apartment more than you like...

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Myron. Hmm... I told you he was old!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh... sit down, sit down. Oh, honey, you know, I once also almost married somebody that I didn’t love. Do you remember Barry?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Humpf, remember him? How we used to make out all the time after you went to sleep.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sometimes just nodding is ok. Uhm, so but anyway, listen, not marrying Barry was the best decision that I ever, ever made. Honey, you deserve true love. Your soulmate is out there, somewhere. Someone that is your age, that is smart , that is fun and that you care about!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You’re right, you’re right! I’m gonna do it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I’m gonna marry Myron and keep looking for Mr Right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, let’s keep talking.

scenes.scene_description: The street in front of Central Perk. Rachel and Amy are walking on the sidewalk.

scenes.location: Madison Square Garden. Mike and Phoebe are walking to their seats

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Excuse me, anniversary. Excuse me, anniversary. . Uhm, sir, could you move your nachos... they’re in my seat. It's my anniversary. Here we are! . Can’t believe it's been a whole year!
lines.stage_direction: looking at her ticket to Mike Mike nods

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I know. This has been the best year... THIS HAS BEEN THE BEST YEAR OF MY LIFE!
lines.stage_direction: the crowd starts cheering so he starts yelling

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: ME TOO! I NEVER THOUGHT I COULD LOVE SOMEONE THIS MUCH!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I FEEL THE SAME WAY!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: YOU’RE SO GENEROUS AND KIND AND YOU'RE AMAZING IN BED IT’S OUR ANNIVERSARY!
lines.stage_direction: crowd stops cheering everyone hears it and stare at them. to everybody

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Knicks fans, please turn your attention to the big screen on the score board. Someone has a special question to ask.
lines.stage_direction: on the screen there’s written ‘Julie, will you marry me?’ and goes on to show a guy kneeling down in front of a girl holding out a ring to her

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh how lame... oh, it’s so tacky, and impersonal.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it’s the worst way to propose!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Excuse me... .
lines.stage_direction: looks strangely shocked he leaves, then Phoebe realizes what she did

scenes.scene_description: Madison Square Garden. Mike and Phoebe are walking to their seats.

scenes.location: Monica’s apartment. Joey enters the room

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, finished my recommendation. Here. And I think you’ll be very, very happy. It’s the longest I ever spent on a computer without looking at porn.
lines.stage_direction: he hands it over to Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t... uh... understand.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Some of the words are a little too sophisticated for ya?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It doesn’t make any sense.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course it does! It’s smart! I used the the-saurus!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: On every word?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yep!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, what was this sentence originally?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, ‘They are warm, nice, people with big hearts’.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And that became ‘they are humid prepossessing Homo Sapiens with full sized aortic pumps...?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah and hey, I really mean it, dude.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Joey, I don’t think we can use this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, because you signed it baby kangaroo Tribbiani . Hey, why don’t you stop worrying about sounding smart and just be yourself!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know what? You don’t need a thesaurus, just write from here, your full sized aortic pump.

scenes.scene_description: Monica’s apartment. Joey enters the room

scenes.location: The corridor. Amy knocks Joey’s door and Rachel opens the door

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Amy, hi!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I took your advice, I left Myron.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, good for you!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I know! I'm Erin Brockovich!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes you are! Oh, I am so proud of you!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Thank you! So, can I stay with you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But Erin Brockovich had her own house.
lines.stage_direction: Joey comes out of Monica’s apartment and sees Rachel and Amy but does not notice the huge amount of bags

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, look who’s back! Why do you have bags? RACH, WHY DOES SHE HAVE BAGS?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, I’m staying with you guys!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: We’re gonna be roomies! Come on!
lines.stage_direction: she snaps her finger and points at Joey, snaps her fingers again and points to the bags

scenes.scene_description: The corridor. Amy knocks Joey’s door and Rachel opens the door.

scenes.location: Joey’s apartment. Rachel is in the living room and Joey comes out of his room

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You slept out here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah... Amy kept kicking me in her sleep yelling ‘Myron, get off!’

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But uhm, we're getting rid of her, right? Rach, please tell me we’re getting rid of her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, I can’t do that!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, come on! Last night I was finishing off a pizza and she said "Uoh oh oh, a moment on the lips, forever on the hips!" I don’t need that kind of talk in my house!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well Joey, uhm look, I know that she’s difficult, but I think it’s really good that she’s here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 'Cause we will appreciate it more when she’s gone?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's just... look, you know, when I first moved to the city I was a lot like her! I was spoiled, self-centered and you guys really took care of me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, Monica made us!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, uhm... whatever, I have really appreciated it, 'cause I don’t think I would be the person that I am today if it wasn’t for you guys. See, I wanna help Amy the way you guys helped me. And I know it’s gonna take patience, but that’s ok.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeaah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Amy, that’s what I was supposed to wear today, that’s why I hung it on the door.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh, sweety, you can’t pull this off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Amy, you know what? I was thinking that maybe now it'd be a good time for us to sit down and, you know, talk about your future.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh, I can’t, honey. I’m gonna go get my eyebrows shaped. I am not happy. Oh... sure you wanna eat that?
lines.stage_direction: points at her eyebrows to Joey who has a pizza box in his hands

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’M CURVY, AND I LIKE IT!

scenes.scene_description: Joey’s apartment. Rachel is in the living room and Joey comes out of his room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi. I just had the worst anniversary ever.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I doubt that! Tell her about us last year.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, well, I bought Chandler a five hundred dollar watch and he wrote me a rap song.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Seriously?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Word!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, mine was worse than that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what happened?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We were at the game, and this guy proposed to his girlfriend on the big screen thing...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that is so tacky.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, that’s what I said, but it turns out, Mike was planning on proposing to me that same way last night!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, Mike was gonna propose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, that’s huge!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, do you wanna marry him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I really do! Yes, but, after I dumped on the way he was gonna propose to me, I don’t think he’s ever gonna ask again! I mean, I said no in Barbados and now this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She's right! If I were a guy and... Did I just say if I were a guy..?
lines.stage_direction: stops himself mid-sentence...everyone stares at him

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe you don't need him to propose to you, maybe you can propose to him!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I don't know, I don't know, isn't that a little desperate?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I proposed to Chandler! Alright, moving on...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I don't think it was desperate, I think it was amazing!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, do you think I should propose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think it could be kind of great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Absolutely! You'll love the feeling! There's nothing like it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, ok, so how should I do it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How about at a game, on the big screen?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uuuh!! How about at a Footlocker?
lines.stage_direction: Sarcastically claps her hands together, faking excitement

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? what? He obviously thinks that's a nice way to be proposed to, plus he'd never suspect it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, that does make sense. Ok, now, would... would you two like that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sounds good to me... but what would a guy think?

scenes.scene_description: Monica’s Apartment. Rachel, Ross, Monica and Chandler are there, Phoebe enters the room

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Nana is on the phone
lines.stage_direction: To Rachel Hands the phone over to Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! That's interesting, since she died seven years ago!!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: She did? Who got her condo in Boca?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello? Oh, it's our nanny! Hi! Oh... God! I hope you feel better! Ok, bye! That's Molly, she's sick. Can you watch Emma today?
lines.stage_direction: Into the phone announces to Amy goes back to the phone conversation Hangs up To Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I can't. I have back-to-back classes. Did Molly say what she had? Because my throat's been hurting?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Menstrual cramps.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't think that's what this is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can any of you watch Emma?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, sorry sweety..

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I've got work and then I'm proposing..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great, shoot, what are we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, I can do it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, actually...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, can I talk to you for a sec.?
lines.stage_direction: He interrupts her immediately, and drags her by her arm to the other side of the room

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um, I do not want her baby-sitting our child.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, for one thing, she keeps calling her Ella!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wha.. well, Ella's a nice name!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, we'll call the next one Ella.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wha... the next one?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, um... I don't want her watching our baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I am trying to help her become a better person. This is a huge breakthrough for her! She just offered to do something for another human being!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I... I don't know..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I'm telling you, she's giving up getting her eyebrows shaped to do this alright? Do you understand how important that is in our world?
lines.stage_direction: points at her own to emphasize the word

scenes.scene_description: Amy walks in carrying a phone handset

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: um... listen, I couldn't help but overhear... 'cause I was trying to... Listen, let me do this alright? I really wanna help you guys out, and plus Rachel's been so wonderful to me...

scenes.scene_description: Amy approaches from behind

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh! Great! So how much does it pay?
lines.stage_direction: very excited Ross just gives up and leaves

scenes.scene_description: Rachel looks at Ross and her agrees silently

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe! How's the second draft of the letter coming?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great, I'm finished! In fact, I just dropped it off at the agency.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Joey is inside, Chandler and Monica walk in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You dropped it off?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can we read it? Can you print out another copy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No can do amigo. No, I didn't use the computer. Felt more personal to hand-write it.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler and Monica look even more shocked

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You hand-wrote it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and don't worry. I didn't try to sound smart at all ! See ya later!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, oh my God, that letter is gonna go in our file! We're never gonna get a kid. No, we're gonna be one of those old couples that collects orchids or has a lot of birds!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's ok, it's ok. You know what? I'll just call the agency and tell them to throw out the letter.
lines.stage_direction: Takes out his mobile starts dialing

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello, this is Chandler Bing. Somebody just dropped off a handwritten recommendation letter, and.. Uh-huh... Uh-huh... okay... thank you. Good-bye. .
lines.stage_direction: on the phone listens hangs up looking very confused

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh, we're screwed, aren't we? You know what? Just tell me on the way to the bird store.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They loved it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They thought it was very smart of us to have a child write the recommendation letter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They thought Joey was a child?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She guessed 8, 9, based on his drawings .

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica look shocked

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, what did you guys do today?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ella wanted to go out, so we went shopping and got some sushi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That sounds like fun.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Yeah, not really. Babies are dull.

scenes.scene_description: Amy comes in with Emma in a stroller

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Hi, how's my girl?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I'm fine! And, I got you a present for letting me stay with you. Ready?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel comes in, sees Amy and Emma

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ta-daaa!

scenes.scene_description: Amy takes off Emma's hat

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You pierced her ears!?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Doesn't it make her nose look smaller?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel looks at Emma

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You pierced her ears? How could you do this without telling me?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, if I had told you, then it wouldn't have been surprise, now would it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think she looks cute. ... but I am wrong!
lines.stage_direction: Rachel turns around and stares at him angrily

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, Oh my God, here comes Ross. He's gonna flip out.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Why, did something happen to his falafel cart?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh.
lines.stage_direction: takes the hat and covers Emma's head and half her face with it

scenes.scene_description: Rachel looks very shocked

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: Monica and Chandler
lines.text: Hi Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Emma. Oh, why is she wearing her hat so low? She can barely see. .
lines.stage_direction: Wants to take the hat off, but Rachel tries to stop him

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nah, I don't really want her to see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because there are so many terrible sights in this world.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Like war. Or that thing in Joey's refrigerator. Remember? It was in a milk carton but it looked like meat?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come here Oh! There she is! Hi!

scenes.scene_description: Ross enters

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?
lines.stage_direction: sees how strangely Monica, Chandler and Rachel are looking at him

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! What... . Please tell me those are clip-ons.
lines.stage_direction: Back to Emma Looks at her Moves the stroller away from him so he can get a better look at her. He looks at her confused. Finally he realizes the difference and gasps

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, they're real!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did she do this to her? I told you we shouldn't have left Emma with her!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I know, and you were right Ross. You are soo irresponsible I am never letting you baby-sit ever again!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Hey you know what, this kid needs me, okay? She needs to have a cool fun aunt!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm a cool, fun aunt!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: O-Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Monica can be cool and fun at organized indoor projects!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't believe this. All I wanted to do was help you try to figure out what to do with your life and this is how you repay me?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, I don't need you to help me, because I already know what I'm going to do with my life.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah? Since when?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Since today... I am going to be a baby stylist.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?
lines.stage_direction: looks at Ross and then at Amy again in disbelief

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's not a thing !

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well, it should be. I'm gonna help babies learn how to accessorize, what colors to wear, what clothes are slimming...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Babies don't care if they're slim.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Enter Amy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Amy, I ju... I just... I just wanna...

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: What? What are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No more falafel for you!
lines.stage_direction: pointing at Amy, shouting Amy looks at Ross, angrily. Rachel clearly doesn't understand what he meant and looks at Ross who gestures "later".

scenes.scene_description: Rachel looks worried

scenes.location: Madison Square Garden. Phoebe and Mike are watching the game. There's cheering

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Great game, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uhuh, uhuh...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Why do you keep looking at the screen?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm not. I'm praying. Please let the Knicks win... Thank you Thor! Where... where are you going?
lines.stage_direction: looking up Mike is standing up

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Going go to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I think you should wait.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if you don't... if you don't hold it in, you don't get all the nutrients.

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Knicks fans, please turn your attention to the big screen on the scoreboard. Someone has a special question to ask.
lines.stage_direction: We see the screen where it says: "Mike will you marry me?" and then we see Phoebe and Mike on the screen. Phoebe stands up and kneels in front of Mike.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike Hannigan... will you marry me?

lines.speaker: Announcer
lines.text: Get a load of this... She's proposing to him . Guess we know who wears the pants in that family.
lines.stage_direction: people are laughing, while Mike still seems bewildered

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's not very enlightened!
lines.stage_direction: There's booing around them, and Mike sinks in his chair, holding his hand in above his eyes, hoping no-one would recognize him

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, hey! Boo us? Boo you!

scenes.scene_description: Madison Square Garden. Phoebe and Mike are watching the game. There's cheering, but Phoebe seems distracted.

scenes.location: Rachel and Joey's. Joey is in the kitchen and the telephone rings

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello? Yeah, this is Joey Tribbiani... Oh, hi! Well, I'm glad you liked my letter... No my mommy and daddy aren't home right now... Okay, bye bye. She was nice!
lines.stage_direction: picks up the phone looks puzzled hangs up to himself

scenes.scene_description: Rachel and Joey's. Joey is in the kitchen and the telephone rings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, get Amy's bags, she is moving out!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoo-hoo!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You're kicking me out?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You put holes in my baby's ears!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Yeah well, at least now people will know she is a girl !

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't believe I ever even tried to help you. You are so beyond help.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You know what? Ever since I got here, you have been nothing but negative.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You didn't want me to marry the old guy with the great apartment. Then, I tried to help your daughter to de-emphasize her flaws And suddenly I am the bad guy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, where are those bags?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She has a lot of crap!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You know what? When I moved in here I thought: This is gonna be so great. Just us sisters, back together again like when we were kids, except without that stupid Jill... Oh! Who has gotten fat by the way...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Seriously?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: hm-mmm... Mom said she gained like fifteen pounds.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hips or thighs?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ass and face.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh my God! I thought she was on Atkins.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: She was. Carbs found her... See, this is what I wanted. Two sisters, talking about real stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I can give you that.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You can?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. I just, I kept trying to make you a better person, but you're... you're already a pretty perfect version of what you are.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Thank you. I've got to admit, Emma does look cute.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did you just say Emma?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ugh, I'm sorry... Ella.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel and Amy enter

scenes.location: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That woman at the game didn't know what she was talking about. Mike, obviously you have balls.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: But please, let's just forget the whole thing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I would love it. Consider it forgotten... But just so you know... however and whenever you decide to propose, I promise I'll say yes. Whether... whether, you know, it is in a basketball game, or in sky writing, or you know, like some lame guy in a cheesy movie who hides it in the cake.
lines.stage_direction: the waiter puts a piece of cake on the table

scenes.scene_description: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant.

scenes.location: Mike and Phoebe at a restaurant

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's in the cake, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Where else would lame Mr. No Balls hide it?
lines.stage_direction: puts on a fake smile he takes the ring from the cake, and cleans it with a napkin

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's the matter with me? How do I keep ruining this? I'm sorry, I'm sorry!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No! It's my fault. I keep trying to propose in these stupid ways and all I wanna do is tell you that I love you and I want to spend the rest of my life with you.

lines.text: I'm gonna do this now .

lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.text: Phoebe, I...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait! Oh wait! Oh no!
lines.stage_direction: she takes off a ring that was already on her left ring finger. After that Mike starts to kneel again, but then... She was wearing rings on all her fingers and her thumb, and takes all of these off.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ready?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Phoebe, I love you. There's no-one else in the world I would ask to marry me... three times. But I wanna take care of you, have babies with you, and grow old with you... Phoebe Buffay, will you marry me?

lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I love you!

lines.text: I love you more!

lines.text: No, what's he like?

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: It's his dad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh... wow, so he's gotta be...

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Old? Yeah! But he travels a lot, so he's hardly ever there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sweety, I gotta tell ya... it sounds a little bit like you like the apartment more than you like...

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Myron. Hmm... I told you he was old!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh... sit down, sit down. Oh, honey, you know, I once also almost married somebody that I didn’t love. Do you remember Barry?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Humpf, remember him? How we used to make out all the time after you went to sleep.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sometimes just nodding is ok. Uhm, so but anyway, listen, not marrying Barry was the best decision that I ever, ever made. Honey, you deserve true love. Your soulmate is out there, somewhere. Someone that is your age, that is smart , that is fun and that you care about!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You’re right, you’re right! I’m gonna do it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I’m gonna marry Myron and keep looking for Mr Right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, let’s keep talking.

lines.text: Excuse me, anniversary. Excuse me, anniversary. . Uhm, sir, could you move your nachos... they’re in my seat. It's my anniversary. Here we are! . Can’t believe it's been a whole year!

lines.text: I don’t... uh... understand.

lines.text: It doesn’t make any sense.

lines.text: Yep!

lines.text: Alright, what was this sentence originally?

lines.text: Oh, ‘They are warm, nice, people with big hearts’.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And that became ‘they are humid prepossessing Homo Sapiens with full sized aortic pumps...?

lines.text: Yeah, yeah and hey, I really mean it, dude.

lines.text: Hey Joey, I don’t think we can use this.

lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, because you signed it baby kangaroo Tribbiani . Hey, why don’t you stop worrying about sounding smart and just be yourself!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know what? You don’t need a thesaurus, just write from here, your full sized aortic pump.

lines.text: Yeah... Amy kept kicking me in her sleep yelling ‘Myron, get off!’

lines.text: But uhm, we're getting rid of her, right? Rach, please tell me we’re getting rid of her.

lines.text: Joey, I can’t do that!

lines.text: Oh, come on! Last night I was finishing off a pizza and she said "Uoh oh oh, a moment on the lips, forever on the hips!" I don’t need that kind of talk in my house!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well Joey, uhm look, I know that she’s difficult, but I think it’s really good that she’s here.

lines.text: 'Cause we will appreciate it more when she’s gone?

lines.text: No, it's just... look, you know, when I first moved to the city I was a lot like her! I was spoiled, self-centered and you guys really took care of me.

lines.text: Yeah, Monica made us!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, uhm... whatever, I have really appreciated it, 'cause I don’t think I would be the person that I am today if it wasn’t for you guys. See, I wanna help Amy the way you guys helped me. And I know it’s gonna take patience, but that’s ok.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeaah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Amy, that’s what I was supposed to wear today, that’s why I hung it on the door.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh, sweety, you can’t pull this off.

lines.text: Oh, I can’t, honey. I’m gonna go get my eyebrows shaped. I am not happy. Oh... sure you wanna eat that?

lines.text: I’M CURVY, AND I LIKE IT!

lines.text: You know what? When I moved in here I thought: This is gonna be so great. Just us sisters, back together again like when we were kids, except without that stupid Jill... Oh! Who has gotten fat by the way...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hips or thighs?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ass and face.

lines.text: Oh! Oh my God! I thought she was on Atkins.

lines.text: Yeah. I just, I kept trying to make you a better person, but you're... you're already a pretty perfect version of what you are.
