lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: After you, miss Banana Hammock. Phoebe : Thank you, mister Bag.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Phoebe and Mike are leaving.

scenes.location: Monica and Chandler's apartment. Ross enters

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, is Rachel here? Monica : No. Ross : She's still at dinner? Monica : I guess. Why? Who's she with? Ross : That guy Mark. From Bloomingdale's... She thinks he's just being nice to her. But I know he really wants to sleep with her. Chandler : It's seven years ago. My time machine works! Ross : We ran into him on the street today and he said he might have a job for her. But I know he just wants to get into her pants. Monica : So what if he wants to sleep with her? I mean, she's single and he's cute. Chandler : Excuse me? Monica : Oh please! Yesterday on the subway? You couldn't stop staring at that woman with the big breasts the whole time . Chandler : For your information, I was staring at her baby. We're about to be parents. Monica : Oh, sorry!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's apartment. Ross enters.

scenes.location: Joey and Rachel's. Joey's on the phone

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean, this soap opera is a great gig, but... am I missing opportunities? You know, I've always thought of myself as a serious actor. I mean, should I be trying to do more independent movies? Mackenzie : I don't know... You know what? I'm gonna put you on with my bear. Hold on. Joey : Hey bear, I need some career advice. THE END
lines.stage_direction: at the other end of the line she puts the phone at the bears ear

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's. Joey's on the phone.

scenes.location: Chandler and Monica's apartment. They are having a diner party with Phoebe and Mike

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Thank you guys for having us over.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Yeah, this is fun, couples night.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I don't know why we hang out with married couples more often.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, because every time we do, you make jokes about swinging and scare them away.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean that Portuguese couple? Yeah, like you wouldn't have done it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you guys... I have great news.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, we're kind of in the middle of diner here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, well, er, I already ate, but sure...! Guess what happened at work today...
lines.stage_direction: they all look at each other when Ross grabs a plate

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A dinosaur died a million years ago?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Try sixty-five million years ago, and then try sssshhhhhh.... My tenure review board met today and I hear it's looking really good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Do you have any idea what this means in academic circles, uh? I am gonna get laid .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi you guys.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, Italian!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No one wanted seconds, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no. I-I'm good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey you guys... You're never gonna believe it. This headhunter called me. I have a meeting tomorrow with Gucci. Gucci wants me .

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm up for tenure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You too! What are the odds?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Guess what? I finally got that seed out of my teeth.
lines.stage_direction: enters they all look expectantly at him

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know who I'm happiest for...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I do , he's been working on that all day!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica's apartment. They are having a diner party with Phoebe and Mike.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Mon? Was it weird changing your name to Geller-Bing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no. It felt nice to acknowledge this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where did you go to do it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uhm the... the ministry... of names... bureau...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: YOU NEVER DID IT!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry. It's just the idea of being an official Bing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! I will have you know that... aah, who am I kidding. Let's call the kid Geller and let Bing die with me.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Here you go.
lines.stage_direction: walks to the couch with coffee for Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks! Honey, would you want me to take your name?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, it's just... It's up to you. It's your name. You've got to live with it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, let's see, call me mrs Hannigan.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mrs Hannigan?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? Can't you see I'm in the middle of something? Ooh, I like it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe! We've got a couple of things we've got to check out at the new house. You want to come with us?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. I know you're not happy about us moving, but you're the only one who hasn't seen the house.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, come with us. You'll see how close it is to the city.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But no, it's not close. You said it was in escrow? I couldn't even find it on the map.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, please come. It would mean so much to us.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know what? You are my friends, I wanna be supportive, I will come with you. SHOTGUN!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Damn it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See you guys later.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll pick you up at eleven. So glad you're coming.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good for you. That was really mature.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? No, the only reason I'm going to their stupid new house, is so I can point out everything that's wrong with it, so they don't move. I'm gonna make them stay here.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You're a strange kind of grown-up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, you can't make someone do something they don't want to do. Believe me, there's something I've been trying to get Mike to do in bed and there's... he's just...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Woo-wo-hey-hey-hey... Can we not talk about that right now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, prude ... Look, Monica and Chandler really love this house. You are not gonna talk them into staying here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey... I can convince people to do anything, you know. I bet I can even get Mike to do that "thing". What is it?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Phoebe, Monica and Chandler on their couch.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I AM NOT GONNA HELP YOU DO THAT! Goodbye!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe whispers something in Joey's ear, but after hearing it he jumps up, shocked

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, I'm here to see mr Campbell... with Gucci. The reservation is probably under Gucci. It's spelled like Gu kk i, which could be confusing.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: Mr Campbell's not here yet. Let me show you to his table.

scenes.scene_description: A restaurant. Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! That's my boss. You have to seat us somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: I'm sorry. That's always mr Campbell's table.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But my... but my boss cannot see me. I'm interviewing for another job.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: I know. With Gu kk i

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sssshhhh!

lines.speaker: Mr Zellner
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi... I'm on a date...

lines.speaker: Mr Zellner
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it is. Yeah, you know, it's tough. Single mom, career... You gotta get out there.

lines.speaker: Mr Zellner
lines.text: Well, you got uhm... good energy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, hi!

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: James Campbell...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Excuse us.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Please...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Oh, yeah... Oh he's cute!

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: So... your resumé is quite impressive.
lines.stage_direction: Mr Zellner who sits behind Rachel shrugs

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wha... My resumé? I wouldn't... I wouldn't call my online dating profile a resumé.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Dating profile? I-I-I'm talking about the work resumé.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whatever happened to just singing for no reason? Huh?
lines.stage_direction: starts singing la la la la

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Maybe people... found it weird.... So, why do you want to leave Ralph Lauren?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? I-I don't.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: You don't?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I-I-I love it there.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: Well, if you don't want to leave, why are we having this lunch?

scenes.scene_description: They walk to the table, but Rachel suddenly gasps. Sitting there is mr Zellner, her boss from her current job at Ralph Lauren.

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: What?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel mimes and mouths to mr Campbell "That is my boss", pointing to mr Zellner

lines.speaker: Mr Campbell
lines.text: That's Hugo Boss?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel now silently whispers "That's my boss".
Rachel holds her hand in order to support her head. Mr Zellner obviously overheard the conversation.

scenes.location: A counter at a government building. Phoebe's waiting in line

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This place is so depressing. If I had to work here I'd kill myself. But you obviously haven't.
lines.stage_direction: to the woman behind her she turns around and the clerk behind the counter heard her.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I need to change my name, please. See, I need to change it because I'm-I'm hiding from the law. You're fun.
lines.stage_direction: the clerk shows no change in expression whatsoever

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: You need to fill out this form.
lines.stage_direction: motions for the next person in line

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well, I just don't, I don't know how it works exactly. See, my name is Buffay and my husband's name is Hannigan, so is it supposed to be Buffay-Hannigan or Hannigan-Buffay?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: It can be anything you want.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, not anything , I mean...

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Yeah... anything .

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this could take a while.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Get out of my line.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: A counter at a government building. Phoebe's waiting in line.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Chandler and Monica are there when Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, not anymore. I changed it today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, mrs Hannigan.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wrong again! Apparently you can change it to anything you want. So I thought, all right, here's an opportunity to be creative. So meet Princess Consuela Banana Hammock.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what we were gonna name the baby.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh! Princess Consuela.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You seriously changed your name to that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, so from now on we have to call you Princess Consuela?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uhm, no. I'm gonna have my friends call me Valerie.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Chandler and Monica are there when Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, how'd the interview go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! It's not good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I always feel that way after an interview. I'll bet it went better than you think.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I didn't get the job at Gucci and I got fired from Ralph Lauren.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is a bad interview.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you, what are you talking about? How did this happen?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, my boss was at the same restaurant where I was having my interview and he heard everything. So later he calls me to his office and he tells me that he's gonna have to let me go, because I'm not a team player. And I said "Wait a minute! Yes I am." and I had to sit there for 45 minutes while he proved that that in fact... was true.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God. I'm so sorry.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel enters, looking depressed

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Wha-hoo! What's this? Well it's a, it's a bottle of champagne. Why is this here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I guess it's here because I GOT TENURE!

lines.speaker: All (except Rachel)
lines.text: Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is the single greatest day of my professional career. Gunther, six glasses!

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Six? You want me to join you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I thought Joey was here. Five is good. Well, I'm gonna have a loogie in my coffee tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh! Israeli champagne. And it's vanilla!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I got tenure. I didn't win the lottery... Hey Rach, so uh... how did your thing go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh it... good! Yeah, but I'm not gonna hear from that for a couple of days.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you know what? You're gonna get it. I-I-I-I can feel it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Can you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, all right. Here's to Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And-and to years of hard work finally paying off.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And to knowing that your career doesn't mean everything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But also knowing it means a lot .

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But more importantly to full well-rounded lives.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...that center around work.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To Ross!

lines.speaker: All (except Rachel)
lines.text: Ross!

scenes.scene_description: Ross enters with a bottle in his hand

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know what the best part about this is? I can never be fired.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No seriously. I have job security for life. You know, I never have to worry. Oh, look at you. Look how happy you are for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's not that. I got fired today. And I didn't get the other job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great. I feel like an idiot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's okay, you didn't know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh... Little heads-up would have been nice.

scenes.scene_description: they all drink from the champagne, but clearly dislike the taste of it

scenes.location: Chandler and Monica's future house. They enter the living room with the realtor and Joey

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you for letting us see the house again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And thank you for explaining to us what escrow means... I've already forgotten what you said, but thank you.

lines.speaker: Realtor
lines.text: Take as long as you want. Just let me know when you're through.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah, so glad you decided to come.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too. Yeah, this place is great. I'm so happy for you guys. Although, you know, I hope you like fungus.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fungus! Yeah. Place is full of it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it's not. We had an inspection and they didn't find anything.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Then I guess I have dry eyes and a scratchy throat for no reason.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe because it's you hung your head out of the window like a dog the whole ride here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe. So this is the living room huh? Ooh, it's pretty dark.
lines.stage_direction: starts feeling around him like he's in a completely dark room, touching Chandler, who backs out and hits him

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it's not!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kiddin'? I think I just saw a bat in the corner!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When your head was hanging out the window, it didn't hit a mailbox, did it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe. Well, I just think you guys can do better than this house, you know? Or any other house for that matter.
lines.stage_direction: glares at him for a moment, then admits grudgingly

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh Joey, look, we know you're having a hard time with this, but we really, we love it here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: FINE, ok, if you love this house so much, then you should just live here, okay? I just hope you get used to that weird humming sound. Hmmmmmmmmmmmmmm...
lines.stage_direction: He turns his back to them and starts humming

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, we know that's you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: no... hmmmmm... it's not... hmmmmmmmmmm

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica's future house. They enter the living room with the realtor and Joey.

scenes.location: Phoebe is at Central Perk. Mike enters

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Welcome back!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ah! I missed you

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, me too!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So, what's new?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm no longer Phoebe Buffay.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: That's great! You changed you name?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes I did! Meet: Princess Consuela Banana Hammock!
lines.stage_direction: She smiles from ear to ear

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You're kidding right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You really did that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yep.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, but you can't do that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why? It's fun, it's different, no-one else has a name like it.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Alright, then I'm gonna change my name.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great, okay, what are you gonna change it to?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Crap Bag.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike Crap Bag?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No, no Mike, just Crap Bag. First name Crap, last name Bag.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're not serious, right?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, I'm serious. It's fun, it's different and no-one else has a name like that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uhu, uhu, well, then, great . If you love it, I love it.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I do love it, and I love your name. I love Princess Consuela.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And I love Crap.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe is at Central Perk. Mike enters.

scenes.location: Joey is in Monica and Chandler's future house

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ow!
lines.stage_direction: turns the card around, obviously had the wrong answer

scenes.scene_description: Joey is in Monica and Chandler's future house, sitting in a child's bedroom, looking at a quiz card which has "5+10=" printed on one side.

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hi, I'm Joey. My stupid friends are buying this house. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: I'm Mackenzie. My stupid parents are selling this house.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: I hate my parents.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I hate my friends. Alright, look. There's gotta be a way that we can stop this from happening.
lines.stage_direction: They shake on it as if they just made a pact

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhm... oh! Okay. You come with me, and you tell them that the house is haunted!

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: What are you? Eight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Woah, uh! Okay, let's hear your great idea.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: I don't have any great ideas. I am eight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh! There's gotta be a way. I mean, you know, if Monica and Chandler move out here and now Phoebe is married to Mike. That just leaves me and Ross and Rach, you know what I mean?

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: I really don't.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What am I gonna do, I feel like I'm losing my friends.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: My parents say I'm gonna make new friends.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, sure, easy for you, you're young. Me, I'm set in my ways.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: This is what my mom was talking about. Whiners are wieners. Look, you want your friends to be happy, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, I guess.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: Well, if moving here is gonna make them happy, don't you want them to do it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, maybe.

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: Then you gotta let them go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I hate to admit it, but you're probably right. How did you get to be so smart?
lines.stage_direction: In near tears, realizes she is right

lines.speaker: Mackenzie
lines.text: I read a lot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Just when I thought we could be friends.
lines.stage_direction: his mood changes instantly he leaves the room

scenes.scene_description: A young girl enters

scenes.location: Outside Ralph Lauren building. Rachel just walked out carrying a box of her stuff

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Hey Rach, I just heard. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thank you...
lines.stage_direction: looks at his face trying to remember his name

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: You still don't know my name, do you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, now I don't have to.
lines.stage_direction: Is embarrassed for a moment, but it quickly passes The man leaves instantly

scenes.scene_description: Outside Ralph Lauren building. Rachel just walked out carrying a box of her stuff, and a strange man approaches her.

scenes.location: Outside Ralph Lauren building. Rachel just walked out carrying a box of her stuff

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, what is taking you so long?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry, it's almost as if this wasn't built for a quick getaway!
lines.stage_direction: stares at her through the door and starts pushing the chair harder, looking very annoyed. He finally manages sarcastic

scenes.scene_description: In the meantime, Ross is trying to squeeze and push a rather large chair through the revolving doors of the Ralph Lauren building.

scenes.location: Outside Ralph Lauren building. Rachel just walked out carrying a box of her stuff

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mark? Oh my God!
lines.stage_direction: turns around puts the box on the chair and they hug each other

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: How've you been?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm fantastic. You remember Ross?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Sure, sure. What's with the chair.
lines.stage_direction: To Ross Rachel signals him not to mention she's been fired

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, you know, you can't always get a seat on the subway, so...

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Clever. So how are you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, you're not catching me on my best day.
lines.stage_direction: looks at her box and chair

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Yeah, a box full of your desk stuff doesn't exactly say big promotion.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, but it's good, you know, I'm gonna take some time off and do some charity work.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Are you sure, because we may have something at Louis Vuitton.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, screw charity work. What've you got?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Why don't we have dinner tonight and talk about it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great! I'll call ya!

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Nice to see you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! yeah, I got tenure!
lines.stage_direction: Mark looks at him strangely and walks off

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, but if we don't get this house, she's stil gonna show up wherever we go! I mean, at least if she's here, it eliminates the element of suprise. I mean, never again will you have to hear the three words that make your balls jump back up inside your body.
lines.stage_direction: She shows this with her index finger, mimicking it pushing something up

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we have to do something. We can't have her living next door. Oh, that does it too.
lines.stage_direction: looks afraid, but at the same time, knows she's right Janice's laughs loudly outside Motions with his index finger like Monica did

scenes.scene_description: The house Monica and Chandler are viewing. Janice comes down the stairs

scenes.location: Mr. Zellner's office. There is a knock on the door

lines.speaker: r Zelner
lines.text: Who is it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's me. Ron . Look, I um, I now Rachel turned you down but I think there is a way you might be able to get her to come back.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: This may surprise you, but re-hiring fired employees, is not my main job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just hear me out. How would you feel about offering her a raise?

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Not good, Ron.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Perhaps I can persuade you. What if you can give your son this genuine pterodactyl egg replica.
lines.stage_direction: Takes a huge egg out of his back whispers

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Wow, that's pretty cool
lines.stage_direction: Takes a long look at the egg while he considers it Takes the egg from Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh? So? We have a deal?

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Okay, you got it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! yes!

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: This is gonna make me very popular.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, Believe me, the ladies, they love it!

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: I meant with my son.
lines.stage_direction: looks at Ross, for a long moment, confused

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good, 'cause the ladies, not so much.
lines.stage_direction: shakes his head, give him a thumbs up and leaves

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Zellner's office. There is a knock on the door.

scenes.location: Joey's apartment. He walks in reading his mail

lines.speaker: Answering machine
lines.text: Joey, this is Al T. Booker, the guy who eats paper. I'm sure you've heard that Estelle passed away. I wanted to let you know there's a memorial for her at the Westside chapel, tomorrow at ten. Hope you can make it.
lines.stage_direction: Looks annoyed that he called him Looks shocked

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God. Hello?
lines.stage_direction: Still in shock when his mobile rings, he picks it up

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, it's Estelle.
lines.stage_direction: on the other side of the line, still pretending to be Estelle Joey's eyes bulge up, he looks afraid

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Estelle?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I wanted to call and tell you that there's no hard feelings for firing me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok-ay. I just, I can't believe you're calling me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I didn't think I should just drop by...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no! Don't drop by, don't drop by!
lines.stage_direction: Starts looking around the room, terrified

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Anyway, you did what you had to do. I'm okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can I ask you something? Uhm, what's it like there?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's alright. Look kiddo, I gotta go. Good luck with the career. You're gonna be huge.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks for everything Estelle. Bye. "out of area". Boy, I'll say.
lines.stage_direction: Hangs up and reads the phone display

scenes.scene_description: Joey's apartment. He walks in reading his mail, and plays back his messages on the answering machine

scenes.location: The house next door to Chandler and Monica's new house. Chandler is pacing worriedly through the living room when Janice enters

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Well... I just talked to Sid, we are definitely putting in an offer on the house... a-a-and I'll bet we get it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The Hitlers will be so disappointed.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: All right, I got to run. Tell Monica I say goodbye. And... I'll see you later, neighbor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait! I just want you to know that... I'm so happy you're going to be here.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, me too...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because... that way... we can pick up where we left off.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I never stopped loving you.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh... my...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah! I want you... I need you... I must have you Janice Litman Goralnik Neihosenstein.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler, what are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now that you live next door, we can be together every day. Sid and Monica never have to know a thing.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I don't know what to say... I mean, you know, obviously we have this... heat between us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Obviously.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: But I love my husband. And I know you love your wife. Now, I don't think we should get this house now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't say that. Don't tangle the dream and take it away.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler, one of us has got to be strong .

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Although, maybe just... one last moment of weakness... Goodbye Chandler Bing.
lines.stage_direction: she kisses Chandler flat on the mouth. Chandler squirms. When she's finished, he looks at her lovingly but uneasily. She leaves

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They're never coming down now.

scenes.scene_description: The house next door to Chandler and Monica's new house. Chandler is pacing worriedly through the living room when Janice enters.

scenes.location: Ross's apartment. Ross is working on his laptop when Rachel enters with Emma

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are never going to believe what happened to me today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ralph Lauren called again and they offered me more money.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They did?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. It was the weirdest thing. Zelner called me and he said we'll do everything we can to get you back. And that I should thank some Ron... I don't even know what department that guy's in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, what are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I took it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's great! So you're staying in New York!

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: YAY! WHOO! YAY!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're excited, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ye-ah. Yeah! You know, the money's great . It's certainly the easier choice...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you know, was I looking forward to going to Paris? Sure. You know, was I excited about working in the fashion capital of the world? Ooh, absolutely... Oh...! Yeah, but you know, this is... it's fine. I'm fine going back to a job where I've pretty much gotten everything out of that I possibly can...
lines.stage_direction: she sits down, and Ross who is stunned to hear all this follows her example

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm, I hadn't no you... I had no idea you were so excited about Paris. Uhm, I mean, you said you were scared.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well yeah, but I mean, it was good scared though, you know? Like when I-moved-to-New-York scared. Or uhm, when I-found-out-I-was-gonna-have-Emma scared... But this is... fine. This is gonna be good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You should go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's what you want. You should go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You really think so?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I really do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I already told Zelner that I would come back...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sure he'll understand.
lines.stage_direction: picks up a skull of some carnivore from his side table and puts it in the same bag in which he brought the pterodactyl egg to Zelner

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. ALL RIGHT! I'm gonna do it. I'm gonna go to Paris.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! I'm going to Paris. Thank you, Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh, I'm so happy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then I'm happy too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you all for coming. We're here today to pay respects to a wonderful agent and a beautiful woman... ..inside. As Estelle's only two clients we would like to say a few words. Dude, where's my speech? That is entertaining. Al Zebooker everybody. THE END
lines.stage_direction: Joey looks at the photograph Joey looks for his notes. The man next to him is chewing something. the man swallows something and looks at Joey. he applauds and Al shows that there's nothing left in his mouth.

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Previously on Friends...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How did the job stuff go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He offered me one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know what? This calls for a bottle of Israels finest.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The job is in Paris.
lines.stage_direction: we see Ross stare in disbelief

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, God! Please, somebody say something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So if you take this job you'll be moving to Paris?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Or facing a bitch of a commute.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, it's huge, and it's scary, and it's... really far, far away from you guys, but this is such an incredible opportunity for me. And I've already talked to them about our situation with Emma, and they said they'll do whatever we need to make us feel comfortable.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean, I'll fly back and forth, they'll fly you out... Anything we want.

lines.text: My boss said I might be getting a new lamp in my cubicle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, we'll work it out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah! You sure this is what you want?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think it is.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I got a really incredible job offer.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, great! All right!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good for you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's in Paris.

lines.text: What? No, no, no! No, no... no... no, no... No, too much is changing, okay? First, Phoebe getting married Congratulations! ... and then these two move into a stupid house in the stupid suburbs...

lines.text: Hey, this afternoon you said you'd be supportive...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, it comes and goes. I wouldn't trust it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, you guys... this is really, really important to me. And it means a lot if you could try to get on board.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course we can. Congratulations. Yay! Joey...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no. My hugs are reserved for people STAYING IN AMERICA.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, it would mean so...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! No! Get your France-going-arms away from me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You okay with this?

lines.text: Well, it makes me feel sad, but...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Talking to Ross.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I see.

lines.text: Well, Rachel moving to another country? Not being able to see her every day. How can I be okay with this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, but what are we gonna do? She really needs this job.

lines.text: How is that gonna happen?

lines.speaker: Chandler

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.text: You mean the guy who kept calling you Ron?

lines.text: I didn't say we were brothers.

lines.text: Hey Phoebe. Hey, tell me what you think. All right. The house next door to the one that we're buying in Westchester? Just went on the market. I wanna take a look at it, but Chandler doesn't.

lines.text: We close escrow tomorrow, so seeing another house can only confuse us, and we're easily confused. We're not very bright.

lines.text: But what if it is better than ours? Should we at least look?

lines.text: What do you think Pheebs?

lines.text: Well, I think that shirt makes you look like you should work at a Baskin Robbins... Anyway... Hey, isn't Joey's agent Estelle Leonard?

lines.text: Yeah, she was just an agent.

lines.text: What if he reads it in the paper?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Unless Snoopy says it to Charlie Brown, I think we're okay.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.text: Uh... What can I do for you?

lines.text: Ah, did she ask you to come here and do this?

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.speaker: it

lines.text: But I'm sorry. There's nothing I can do... Ah, it's not true, there is... nothing I want to do.

lines.text: Yeah, his name is Ross. What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, nothing, it’s just, it’s close to Ron. Does he.. Does little Ross like dinosaurs by any chance?

lines.text: Yeah, they're all he talks about, why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How would he like to come with me to the Museum of Natural History after everyone else has left, just the two of us, and he can touch anything he wants. . I just heard it as you must have heard it and that’s not good. Let me start again. I’m a paleontologist, you’ll be there with us and the touching refers only to bones… fossils!

lines.text: You can really arrange that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You let Rachel come back, and it’s done.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Well, I guess having Rachel back wouldn’t be the worst thing in the world.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Yes! Thank you! This is great. Thank you so much. And I swear, your kid is going to have the time of his life.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: That’s great. I worry about little Ross. He’s always reading, he's collecting rocks and he’s obsessed with dinosaurs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He’ll be fine.

lines.text: Because a promise between friends means never having to give a reason.

lines.text: Hello?

lines.text: No, no, look. All I’m saying is that you’re my agent, ok? And you’re not getting me into any auditions and I’m tired of it.

lines.text: What are you saying?

lines.text: I’m saying that… . This isn't working for me anymore, ok? Estelle, you’re fired. Goodbye. .

lines.text: I d on't know why.

lines.text: Ooh, that decides it then. I was on the fence . But knowing that you two would be our neighbors? Ah! now we have to get it! Ellen, we're going to talk numbers.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler and Monica are utterly shocked Grabs Ellen by her elbow and pulls her outside

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This can not be happening!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, the realtor said another couple made an offer. Maybe the Janice's won't get it! Maybe the other couple will.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The only way that that is going to happen, is if the other couple are the Hitlers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, Okay, All right. What if we got both houses? Huh? We can turn this house into a guest house.
lines.stage_direction: Thinks a little more clapping her hands

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is a great idea! And by the way, I don't mean to sound distasteful, but when did you start crapping money!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, you come up with an idea.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright alright, we still have three hours till escrow closes on our house. We can still get out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But we love our house.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Will we love it so much with her next door? And she's gonna be louder out here too. Just the crickets and "Oh My God"!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, but if we don't get this house, she's stil gonna show up wherever we go! I mean, at least if she's here, it eliminates the element of suprise. I mean, never again will you have to hear the three words that make your balls jump back up inside your body.
lines.stage_direction: She shows this with her index finger, mimicking it pushing something up

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we have to do something. We can't have her living next door. Oh, that does it too.
lines.stage_direction: looks afraid, but at the same time, knows she's right Janice's laughs loudly outside Motions with his index finger like Monica did

scenes.scene_description: The house Monica and Chandler are viewing. Janice comes down the stairs

scenes.location: Mr. Zellner's office. There is a knock on the door

lines.speaker: r Zelner
lines.text: Who is it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's me. Ron . Look, I um, I now Rachel turned you down but I think there is a way you might be able to get her to come back.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: This may surprise you, but re-hiring fired employees, is not my main job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just hear me out. How would you feel about offering her a raise?

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Not good, Ron.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Perhaps I can persuade you. What if you can give your son this genuine pterodactyl egg replica.
lines.stage_direction: Takes a huge egg out of his back whispers

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Wow, that's pretty cool
lines.stage_direction: Takes a long look at the egg while he considers it Takes the egg from Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh? So? We have a deal?

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Okay, you got it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! yes!

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: This is gonna make me very popular.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, Believe me, the ladies, they love it!

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: I meant with my son.
lines.stage_direction: looks at Ross, for a long moment, confused

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good, 'cause the ladies, not so much.
lines.stage_direction: shakes his head, give him a thumbs up and leaves

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Zellner's office. There is a knock on the door.

scenes.location: Joey's apartment. He walks in reading his mail

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God. Hello?
lines.stage_direction: Still in shock when his mobile rings, he picks it up

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, it's Estelle.
lines.stage_direction: on the other side of the line, still pretending to be Estelle Joey's eyes bulge up, he looks afraid

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Estelle?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I wanted to call and tell you that there's no hard feelings for firing me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok-ay. I just, I can't believe you're calling me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I didn't think I should just drop by...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no! Don't drop by, don't drop by!
lines.stage_direction: Starts looking around the room, terrified

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Anyway, you did what you had to do. I'm okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can I ask you something? Uhm, what's it like there?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's alright. Look kiddo, I gotta go. Good luck with the career. You're gonna be huge.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks for everything Estelle. Bye. "out of area". Boy, I'll say.
lines.stage_direction: Hangs up and reads the phone display

scenes.location: The house next door to Chandler and Monica's new house. Chandler is pacing worriedly through the living room when Janice enters

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The Hitlers will be so disappointed.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: All right, I got to run. Tell Monica I say goodbye. And... I'll see you later, neighbor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait! I just want you to know that... I'm so happy you're going to be here.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, me too...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because... that way... we can pick up where we left off.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I never stopped loving you.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh... my...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah! I want you... I need you... I must have you Janice Litman Goralnik Neihosenstein.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler, what are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now that you live next door, we can be together every day. Sid and Monica never have to know a thing.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I don't know what to say... I mean, you know, obviously we have this... heat between us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Obviously.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: But I love my husband. And I know you love your wife. Now, I don't think we should get this house now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't say that. Don't tangle the dream and take it away.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler, one of us has got to be strong .

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Although, maybe just... one last moment of weakness... Goodbye Chandler Bing.
lines.stage_direction: she kisses Chandler flat on the mouth. Chandler squirms. When she's finished, he looks at her lovingly but uneasily. She leaves

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They're never coming down now.

scenes.scene_description: The house next door to Chandler and Monica's new house. Chandler is pacing worriedly through the living room when Janice enters.

scenes.location: Ross's apartment. Ross is working on his laptop when Rachel enters with Emma

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are never going to believe what happened to me today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ralph Lauren called again and they offered me more money.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They did?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. It was the weirdest thing. Zelner called me and he said we'll do everything we can to get you back. And that I should thank some Ron... I don't even know what department that guy's in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, what are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I took it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's great! So you're staying in New York!

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: YAY! WHOO! YAY!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're excited, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ye-ah. Yeah! You know, the money's great . It's certainly the easier choice...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you know, was I looking forward to going to Paris? Sure. You know, was I excited about working in the fashion capital of the world? Ooh, absolutely... Oh...! Yeah, but you know, this is... it's fine. I'm fine going back to a job where I've pretty much gotten everything out of that I possibly can...
lines.stage_direction: she sits down, and Ross who is stunned to hear all this follows her example

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm, I hadn't no you... I had no idea you were so excited about Paris. Uhm, I mean, you said you were scared.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well yeah, but I mean, it was good scared though, you know? Like when I-moved-to-New-York scared. Or uhm, when I-found-out-I-was-gonna-have-Emma scared... But this is... fine. This is gonna be good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You should go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's what you want. You should go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You really think so?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I really do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I already told Zelner that I would come back...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sure he'll understand.
lines.stage_direction: picks up a skull of some carnivore from his side table and puts it in the same bag in which he brought the pterodactyl egg to Zelner

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. ALL RIGHT! I'm gonna do it. I'm gonna go to Paris.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! I'm going to Paris. Thank you, Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh, I'm so happy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then I'm happy too.

lines.text: Thank you all for coming. We're here today to pay respects to a wonderful agent and a beautiful woman... ..inside. As Estelle's only two clients we would like to say a few words. Dude, where's my speech? That is entertaining. Al Zebooker everybody.

So, enjoy: smoke oyster casserole with a breakfast cereal crust, kidney beans in their own juices, and for dessert, a questionable orange. Joey : Hey. Chandler : Hey. Ross : Hey, where's Rach? Joey : Oh, she's putting Emma down, she'll be over in a second. Ross : Great. Joey : Now it just hit her that she's leaving and she's kind of emotional so no one say anything to set her off, ok? Monica : Yeah.

Chandler : Oh yeah, right! Good luck getting another scarf dance from me! Monica : That would be a terrible punishment. But, I'm serious, I've never seen these before. Chandler : Really? Then what are they doing in our guestroom? Monica : Rachel used to live in that room. Chandler : Rachel... with handcuffs! Interesting! Monica : Joey's bare ass! Chandler : . Well played.

Monica : You're the best friend I ever had. Rachel : Monica : What? Rachel : I... I... I... Monica : That is so sweet.

lines.text: So, I guess you're next. You're ready? Ross : I don't think so. Chandler : Oh, you're definately not. I haven't cried like that in years. Phoebe : I don't know. But, God, Joey seems to be handling it suprisingly well.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Unbelievable. She says goodbye to everyone but me . Monica : Well, maybe she thought that with all of your history it could be, you know, implicit. Ross : Well, it needs to be plicit . Phoebe : You know, maybe she was just really spent from our talk. It was pretty intense. Monica : Yeah. Mine too. Chandler : Mine was a humdinger Ross : O-kay... I mean, don't I deserve anything ? I mean, a few tears, a cursory hug? NOT FROM YOU! Phoebe : Ross, if you're this upset, you should go and talk to her. Monica : And say what? "You owe me a goodbye", I mean, he's got more pride than that. Ross : THE HELL I DO!

lines.text: I hope Ross isn't too upset. Joey : I'm sure he's not more bummed out than I am. Phoebe : Tell me about it. Chandler : Well, you can't say we don't know how to throw a party. Phoebe : All right, I think I'm gonna head out. Monica : Uh, where do you think you're going? Phoebe : I thought I was going home to go to bed, but I'm sensing there's something less fun for me to do here. Monica : We're moving in a couple of days and we've got a lot of packing to do. It would be great if you guys could pitch in. Chandler : Joey and I can finish up in the guest room. Joey : Oh, yeah, yeah, good idea. Monica : Oh no! You and Phoebe are gonna help me in here. Chandler : You couldn't be cool. Monica : Okay, we're gonna start in the kitchen. Plates get put into plate protectors and stacked ten to a box. The silverware gets bundled in rubber bands and then bubble wrapped. Got it? Phoebe and Joey : Yeah. Monica : Good! Now I need you to be careful and efficient. And remember, if I am harsh with you, it is only because you are doing it wrong. Chandler : Hey Mon, I think I figured out whose handcuffs they are. Monica : You did? How? Chandler : Well, I was cleaning out the closet and I found some pictures of them... being used. Monica : Oh my God! Let me see. Joey : Who's that dirty old lady? Chandler : Monica's grandmother. Monica : Nana liked it rough!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You really think I didn't say goodbye to you because I don't care? Ross : That's what it seemed like. Rachel : I cannot believe that after ten years, you do not know ONE thing about me. Ross : Fine, then why didn't you say something? Rachel : Because it is too damn hard Ross. I can't even begin to explain to you how much I'm gonna miss you. When I think about not seeing you every day, it makes me not want to go... Okay, so if you think that I didn't say goodbye to you because you don't mean as much to me as everybody else, you're wrong. It's because you mean more to me. So there, all right, there's your goodbye... Oh! Ross : Rach! Rachel : What? Ross : You keep, you keep... You can't... Rachel : WHAT?

lines.text: Her name is Erica.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aw..

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, that pregnant girl's name was Erica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. It's a shame you two didn't get to spend more time together.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, we named the boy Jack after dad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aw, he's gonna be so happy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Jack Bing. I love that. Ooh, it sounds like a '40s newspaper guy, you know? "Jack Bing, Morning Gazette. I'm gonna blow this story wide open!"

scenes.scene_description: Joey stares at Chandler and Monica and finally puts two and two together. He gasps.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my gosh. Wow, so beautiful.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I want one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah? Well, tell me which one, and I'll try slip it in my coat.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Seriously. Wanna make one of those?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: One? How about a whole bunch?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Ooh, we could teach them to sing, and we can be like the Von Trapp family! Only without the Nazis. Although that sounds kinda dull.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you guys, I can't believe this. But I'll leave now, or I'm gonna miss my plane.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm just so glad you got to see the babies.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica carry Erica and Jack over to the crib and put them down carefully.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Me too. Oh, I'm just sorry I'm not gonna be around to watch you two attempt to handle this! Alright, I can't say goodbye to you guys again. I love you all so much.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Call us when you get there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I will. Ross, come here.

scenes.scene_description: They hug.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just want you to know.. Last night.. I'll never forget it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Neither will I.

scenes.scene_description: She pulls him over to the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, now I really have to go. Okay. Au revoir! Oh, they're gonna really hate me over there.

scenes.scene_description: They hug as Phoebe and Joey stare at the two of them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, you just let her go?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, maybe that's for the best.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. You know? You just... Look, you gotta... You gotta think about last night the way she does, okay? Maybe, maybe sleeping together was the perfect way to say goodbye?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But now she'll never know how he feels!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe that's okay. You know? Maybe, maybe it is better this way? I mean, now, now you can move on. I mean, you've been trying to for so long, maybe now that you're on different continents.. Right?

scenes.scene_description: She leaves.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe now you can actually do it. You know? You can finally get over her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, that's true. Except I don't wanna get over her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't! I wanna be with her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I'm gonna go after her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you are!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Woo!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe nods.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, wait! Get your coat! Get your coat!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My coat...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is so cool!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I have no idea what's going on, but I am excited!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But Ross, Ross. What do you, what do you think she's going to say?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know, but I.. Look, even if she shoots me down, at least I won't spend the rest of my life wondering what would have happened. Where - where is my coat?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You didn't bring one! My cab's downstairs, I'll drive you to the airport.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, guys, wish me luck.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hurry!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good luck, good luck!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler look shocked as Ross goes to leave.
Phoebe and Ross leave.

lines.text: There's no seatbelt!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's okay. If - if we hit anything, the engine will explode, so you know, it's better if you're thrown from the car.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright, alright, let's do this!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay!

scenes.scene_description: Ross looks terrified.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: 18th and East End.

lines.text: I - I don't take passengers.

lines.text: Hey! The law says you have to accept any fare.

lines.text: No, you don't understand. This isn't a real cab.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Alright, I gotta report you. What's your medallion number?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My medallion number is, "Get out of the cab!"

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Get out of the cab!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Get out of the cab!

scenes.scene_description: A guy comes up and gets into the backseat of the cab.
The man jumps out, obviously a little scared. Phoebe drives off.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey, hey, can I give you guys your house-warming present now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now, that you can do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica, Chandler and Joey are there, packing the last boxes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah... Chick Jr.? Duck Jr.? Don't hide from mama!

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Joey's apartment. Joey looks inside the cardboard box that used to be the home of Chick Jr. and Duck Jr., but they have disappeared.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You can open your eyes now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are we off the bridge?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is the old woman on the bicycle still alive?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, she jumped right back up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, Phoebe, slow down!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do you wanna get to Rachel in time?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, but I don't wanna die in your cab!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You should have thought of that before you got in!

scenes.scene_description: Ross opens his eyes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Toll-booth.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hurry!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Ross tries to open a plastic bag filled with quarters, but he's quite slow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Damn, that window is clean.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe tries to throw some quarters out the window, but she has forgotten to open the window, and she and Ross scream.

scenes.location: Joey's apartment. Joey is still looking for the birds

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Quack, quack, tweet, tweet, quack, quack, tweet, tweet, quack, quack, tweet, tweet, quack, quack, tweet, tweet, quack, tweet, quack...

scenes.scene_description: Joey's apartment. Joey is still looking for the birds.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We were wondering what was taking so long with the gift, but now we understand you were doing this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, I wanted to surprise you, but for your house-warming gift, I got you a baby-chick and a baby-duck!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler enter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really? You got us a chick and a duck?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, great! Just what you want for a new house with infants. Bird feces.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, they must have jumped off the table, 'cause now they're gone!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, don't worry, we'll find them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Actually, I'm gonna go check on the twins.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler grins, while Monica is less enthusiastic.

lines.text: Oh God! What did I just step on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh!

lines.text: It's okay, it's just an egg roll.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh..

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You stepped on my egg roll?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry, I didn't know to look for Chinese food on the floor.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Just put it on a plate and leave.

scenes.scene_description: Monica turns around and is about to leave when she steps on something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, let's find these birds.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait, wait. Do you hear that?

scenes.scene_description: Suddenly, they hear the birds.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! They're in the table!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that can't be good!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We gotta get them out of there!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, oh! Maybe we can lure them out. You know any birdcalls?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, tons, I'm quite the woodsman.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, maybe we can just tip the table a little.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey, wait! The ball!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh!

scenes.scene_description: They realise that the birds are in the foosball table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh God! So what do we do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know. Maybe we can open this up somehow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No... It's all glued together.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Does that mean we have to bust it open?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know. Maybe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know! It's.. It's the foosball table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, you know what? We don't have a choice. It's like I would have said in that sci-fi movie if I'd gotten the part. "Those are our men in there, we have to get them out! Even if I have to sacrifice the most important thing in my life; my time-machine."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did that movie ever get made?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It did not.

scenes.scene_description: The ball rolls into one of the goals, and Chandler and Joey listen in horror as the ball makes its way inside the table. Finally, they can hear the birds again.

lines.text: What? We're never gonna make it!

lines.text: Not with that attitude! Now, haul ass!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.text: I have it, I have it, I have it. Oh, okay, I can't find it, but I remember that I was in seat 32C, because that's my bra-size.

lines.text: Okay, fine! But you know what? If I was in 36D, we would not be having this problem.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We're on our honeymoon.

lines.text: I don't care. Whatever is the cheapest.

lines.text: I'm so lucky I married you.

lines.text: Oh! Shoot! Damn it! Where is it? Oh! Oh! I found it! I found it!

lines.text: Hah! I found it! I told you I would find it! In your face! You're a different person.

lines.text: No, did we miss it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe we have the flight-number wrong. God.

lines.text: Oh my God! Ross, you wouldn't believe the cute little noises the twins are making. Listen.

lines.text: Monica? Monica, Monica, Monica, Monica..?

lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Shoot, they were doing it before.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's alright. Listen, listen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, wait, wait, wait! Here they go again.

scenes.scene_description: She holds the phone down to the twins.

lines.text: Monica? Monica, Monica, Monica, Monica..?

lines.text: Isn't that cute?

lines.text: Oh, okay. Alright, it's flight 421. Leaves at 8:40.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, that's what I have. It's not on the board.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't worry, you guys, we're gonna get you out of there.

lines.text: And we're also gonna buy you tiny, bird hearing-aids.

lines.text: Okay. Here goes.

lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I need to say goodbye to the table first.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Table, you have given us so many great times. And you guys, Jordan, Victor, Joel... All of you guys. What can I say? You guys make us look good. You wanna say anything?

lines.text: Well, I can't do it either.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! Did you find them?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, they're stuck inside the table!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We have to bust it open, but neither of us can do it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, well sure. This gotta be so hard. I'll do it. Gimme!

scenes.scene_description: Monica enters.
Monica grabs the hammer and the crowbar and gets ready to bust it open.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe! Wow! No, no, no!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I've never gone this fast before.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, forget it, okay? Newark is - is like an hour away. There's no way we're gonna make it in time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She's got her cell, you could call her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am not doing this over the phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don't have any other choice!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe screams.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel? Oh, good. Hey, by the way, did you just get on the plane?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: For what it's worth, we would have caught her if we were at the right airport.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh, Rach, hang on.

scenes.scene_description: She lets go of the steering wheel to get her cell-phone from her purse. Ross screams and reaches over in order to hold onto the wheel. Cut to the plane. Rachel is sitting in her seat when her cell-phone rings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe? Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uhm, actually no. No, you've... You have to get off the plane.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I have this feeling that something's wrong with it. Something is wrong with the left Philange.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, honey, I'm sure there's nothing wrong with the plane.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe tries to give her phone to Ross, but he won't take it. He mouths "no."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, look, I have to go. I love you, and I will call you the minute I get to Paris.

scenes.scene_description: The passenger in the seat next to Rachel looks at her and seems a little nervous.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that was just my crazy friend. She told me I should get off the plane, because she had a feeling that there was something wrong with the left Philange.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I wouldn't worry about it. She's always coming up with stuff like this, and you know what? She's almost never right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well...

scenes.scene_description: Rachel hangs up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, what are you doing?

lines.text: Excuse me, sir, where are you going?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Could I get some peanuts?

lines.speaker: Air stewardess
lines.text: There's nothing wrong with the plane.

lines.text: There is no Philange!

lines.speaker: Air stewardess
lines.text: Ma'am, please sit down!

scenes.scene_description: The passenger stands up and gets his suitcase from the overhead compartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This is ridiculous! I...

scenes.scene_description: Everybody walks out of the plane.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, okay.

scenes.scene_description: She notices that everybody is leaving.
Rachel leaves as well.

scenes.location: Joey's apartment. Monica has completely destroyed the foosball-table

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright. My job here is done.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was... Impressive.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you didn't even use the tools for most of it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, they were just slowing me down. Alright, I have to get back to the babies. I'll see you girls later.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry about the table, man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You gonna buy a new one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Probably not. Nah. I don't know how much I'm gonna wanna play after you go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, at least we got these little guys out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Aww, we were worried about you! Hm. I guess I better get used to things crapping in my hand, huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm gonna miss these little guys. It was nice having birds around again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you know what? Maybe we should keep them here with you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I mean we've got a lot going on right now. And, plus, here they'd have their own room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I could get a goose!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I - I think you're set with the poultry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks man. Did you hear that, you guys? You're gonna get to stay here! And, and it's good, you know, 'cause, 'cause now you have a reason to come visit.

lines.text: I think there may be another reason. So, awkward hug or lame cool guy handshake?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, lame cool guy handshake, yeah.

They do the lame cool guy handshake. They look at each other, and then they hug.

scenes.scene_description: The gate at the airport. The passengers are standing in line, and they're about to board the plane again.

lines.text: I don't see her.
